response.justification: Bogdan Szabo demonstrates strong software development skills and diverse experience across multiple domains, but his background lacks direct alignment with the core requirements of the role, particularly in C++ for capital markets/fixed income systems. His expertise in full-stack development, AI, and other languages (TypeScript, Node.js, etc.) is impressive but not directly transferable to this specific position.
strengths: Extensive experience in software development with a focus on testability and modern practices (TDD, CI/CD, pair programming).
Strong communication skills and experience in knowledge sharing (workshops, talks, open-source contributions).
Diverse technical background with exposure to multiple languages, frameworks, and domains (e.g., GIS, real-time systems).
concerns: Limited recent C++ experience, especially on Windows platforms (last noted in 2015 at National Instruments).
No evident experience in capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domains.
Primary expertise lies in web/mobile development and scripting languages, not low-latency or financial systems.
